channel.title: NPR Topics: Environment
channel.description: Breaking news on the environment, climate change, pollution, and endangered species. Also featuring Climate Connections, a special series on climate change co-produced by NPR and National Geographic.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: EPA flags microplastics, pharmaceuticals as contaminants in drinking water
item.description: There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.
item.content:encoded:
There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: The oil industry is betting big on plastics. Here's what that means for the future
item.description: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.content:encoded: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Trump administration cuts turned rural towns into sitting ducks for disasters
item.description: The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.

(Image credit: Mel Evans)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Meet the man who walks barefoot — and was born — on an active volcano
item.description: The volcanologist on the island of Vanuatu who walks barefoot over cooled lava fields tells NPR's Don Gonyea how to see nature at its most primeval.
item.content:encoded: The volcanologist on the island of Vanuatu who walks barefoot over cooled lava fields tells NPR's Don Gonyea how to see nature at its most primeval.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Trump's attacks on offshore wind could hurt infrastructure spending across the economy
item.description: President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: DON EMMERT/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
item.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

(Image credit: Andy Wong/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The oldest known recording of a whale song reveals how oceans have changed
item.description: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.content:encoded: Researchers at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have uncovered the oldest known recording of whale song. And it reveals a noisier soundscape of today's oceans.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Moss is an underdog in the world of plants when it comes to federal protection
item.description: Hundreds of plants are protected by the Endangered Species Act, but moss is an underdog. There's only one type that has federal protection, but a group of scientists wants to change that.
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of plants are protected by the Endangered Species Act, but moss is an underdog. There's only one type that has federal protection, but a group of scientists wants to change that.
item.dc:creator: Kate Grumke

item.title: Bringing marine life back to South Florida's 'forgotten edge'
item.description: Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.
item.content:encoded:
Seawalls are great at protecting property and people. A new nature-inspired seawall add-on is trying to make them better at protecting marine wildlife too.

(Image credit: Nathan Rott)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Easy-to-use solar panels are coming, but utilities are trying to delay them
item.description: Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.
item.content:encoded:
Utilities are convincing lawmakers around the U.S. to delay bills that would allow people to buy solar panels, plug them into an outlet and begin generating electricity.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady
